home.title: Jakub Dolének | Full-stack Developer
home.description: Portfolio of Jakub Dolének featuring full-stack projects, automation systems, and collaboration contact.

about.title: About | Jakub Dolének
about.description: Experience, education, and engineering focus of Jakub Dolének as a full-stack developer.

projects.title: Projects | Jakub Dolének
projects.description: Projects by Jakub Dolének, including full-stack applications, automation, web projects, and older game development work.

person.description: Full-stack developer focused on process automation, backend services, and self-hosting in a personal homelab.

hero.description: I work as a <highlight>full-stack developer</highlight>, building <highlight>internal applications</highlight> and <highlight>ERP system</highlight> extensions. I take solutions <highlight>from the first conversation with a user through deployment</highlight> and use them to simplify work for colleagues and customers. Outside work, I build my own projects and run a <highlight>homelab</highlight> on Linux with Proxmox and Docker, including this website.

deadlock-patch-notes.title: Deadlock Patch Notes
deadlock-patch-notes.summary: Community archive for Deadlock updates with structured patch history, hero changes, item timelines, and spell timelines.
deadlock-patch-notes.why: I built it because official changelogs are hard to compare and difficult to navigate.
deadlock-patch-notes.impact: An automated Go ingestion pipeline parses official changelog sources into PostgreSQL read models served by a Go API, Next.js frontend, RSS feeds, and public JSON endpoints.

levne-deskovky.title: Deskovky Levně
levne-deskovky.summary: Czech board-game price tracker that aggregates offers from multiple stores, availability, filters, and per-store price history.
levne-deskovky.why: I wanted a practical overview of board-game prices and how they change over time.
levne-deskovky.impact: Combines a React frontend, Go read API, PostgreSQL read models, optional Redis cache, and SEO prerendering for a production-ready comparison site.

discord-automation.title: Discord Automation
discord-automation.summary: Desktop WPF app that embeds Discord via WebView2 and automates command dispatch through DevTools with DOM polling.
discord-automation.why: The project came from a need to automate repetitive actions that took too much manual time.
discord-automation.impact: Synchronizes cooldowns from bot replies, deduplicates interactive prompts, handles guard/captcha incidents, and provides logs plus a control UI for long-running operation.

kuchar-v-akci.title: Recepty v Akci
kuchar-v-akci.summary: Self-hosted recipe platform inspired by supermarket flyers with structured ingredients and preparation steps.
kuchar-v-akci.why: I wanted to use AI to create recipes only from products currently on sale, saving time in the store and when deciding what to cook.
kuchar-v-akci.impact: A self-hosted n8n pipeline downloads flyer pages, converts them to images, extracts products into Supabase, and generates recipes for the React frontend.

smithing-master-bot.title: Bot using PyAutoGUI
smithing-master-bot.summary: Desktop automation bot using PyAutoGUI and OpenCV template matching to control an Android emulator.
smithing-master-bot.why: I used it as a practical experiment in screen-based automation without direct API access.
smithing-master-bot.impact: Detects on-screen templates and automates battles, rewards, and map progression in an Android emulator.

portfolio.title: Personal Portfolio
portfolio.summary: This site, built with React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS to present work with a credible engineering-first narrative.
portfolio.why: The goal was to own a space that presents projects in more depth than a standard resume.
portfolio.impact: Separates project and profile content into typed data, with bilingual content, dark mode, and a clear project presentation.

mobile-game-unity.title: Mobile Game in Unity
mobile-game-unity.summary: Android RPG prototype in Unity with equipment crafting, level progression, and saved player progress.
mobile-game-unity.why: I built it as my graduation project to practice a complete game loop from mechanics through an Android build.
mobile-game-unity.impact: Delivered a playable Android build with 30 levels, a progression system, equipment inventory, and mobile-screen optimization.

morePage.heading: Projects
morePage.description: Older and supporting projects that show additional technologies, experiments, and automation work outside the featured selection.

contact.description: If you are hiring or building, feel free to reach out.

about.title: About me
about.intro:
Hi! My name is <highlight>Jakub Dolének</highlight>, and I am a full-stack developer and Applied Informatics student at FIM UHK.
I enjoy building applications and tools that save people time and actually get used. At work, I take solutions from the first conversation with a user through technical design to deployment.
In my free time, I build <projectsLink>ambitious projects</projectsLink>, and lately I have been absorbed by <highlight>self-hosting</highlight>. I run a homelab on Linux with Proxmox and Docker, where I practice deployment, automation, and managing my own services.

timeline.title: Full-stack Developer
timeline.description: I design and implement internal automation solutions from initial analysis through deployment. My work focuses on tools that solve concrete operational problems, speed up business processes, and make day-to-day work easier for colleagues. I work with MSSQL daily and translate requirements from client projects into concrete ERP system extensions and customizations, covering both the technical design and implementation.

timeline.title: University of Hradec Králové
timeline.description: I study Applied Informatics with a focus on software engineering. The program extends my practical experience with deeper foundations in software architecture, algorithms, and databases. In team projects, I work on designing, implementing, and testing full-stack applications.

timeline.title: Secondary Technical School Hradec Králové
timeline.description: During my studies, I gained foundations in programming, computer networking, and hardware. I also built my first larger project in C# while studying there.
